items.title: Celtic sign striker Cvancara on loan
items.summary: Celtic have signed striker Tomas Cvancara on loan until the end of the season.

items.title: NAC op hoofdlijnen akkoord over MLS-transfer Valerius
items.summary: Cherrion Valerius (20) is mogelijk bezig aan zijn laatste dagen bij NAC Breda. De talentvolle verdediger geniet namelijk concrete interesse van clubs uit het buitenland. Minnesota United deed al meerdere keren een bod. Dat bevestigen ingewijden.

items.title: Schalke 04 slaat toe: Youri Mulder jubelt na binnenhalen Dzeko
items.summary: Schalke 04 heeft zich versterkt met Edin Dzeko (39). De voormalige topspits moet de Duitse traditieclub helpen te promoveren naar de Bundesliga.

items.title: England announce first post-Ashes change with national selector to leave
items.summary: England men's national selector Luke Wright is to step down from his position following the T20 World Cup in India and Sri Lanka next month.

items.title: Frenkie de Jong en Pedri bezorgen Flick kopzorgen richting CL-apotheose
items.summary: Het middenveld van Barcelona bezorgt Hansi Flick kopzorgen in aanloop naar de Champions League-apotheose. De Catalaanse topclub heeft nog veel om voor te spelen, maar kan in het laatste duel met FC Kopenhagen niet beschikken over Frenkie de Jong en Pedri.

items.title: Szoboszlai: 'I did my homework' on trick free kick
items.summary: Dominik Szoboszlai said he did his "homework" paid off on his trick free kick that gave Liverpool the lead in a 3-0 win at Marseille in the Champions League on Wednesday.

items.title: Barça win, but injury for Pedri 'not good news'
items.summary: Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said it's "not good news" after losing Pedri to a hamstring injury in Wednesday's 4-2 Champions League win away at Slavia Prague.

items.title: L'pool top English club in Money League; Utd fall
items.summary: Real Madrid topped the latest Deloitte Football Money League rankings, generating a record €1.2 billion ($1.4 billion) in revenue, with the top 20 teams represented clearing €12.4 billion ($14.5 billion) when combined, also a record.

items.title: Kane scores 2, misses pen as Bayern make last 16
items.summary: Harry Kane scored twice as 10-man Bayern Munich sealed their place in the Champions League round of 16 with a 2-0 win against Union Saint-Gilloise.

items.title: 'Transfer Deijl past bij trendbreuk van Feyenoord'
items.summary: Go Ahead Eagles-aanvoerder Mats Deijl lijkt te vertrekken naar Feyenoord. In Rondje Europa merkt hoofdredacteur Pieter Zwart op dat Feyenoord met deze transfer doorgaat met de trendbreuk, die na het tijdperk van Frank Arnesen is ingezet.

items.title: The EIGHT strikers who could move in the January window
items.summary: There is a battle going on for several top strikers across Europe.

items.title: We bought a soccer team! What NBA greats Kerr, Nash & Co. learned owning LaLiga's Mallorca
items.summary: Steve Kerr and Steve Nash own Mallorca with a former USMNT international and an ex-tennis pro. What has it taught them about sport, leadership and life?

items.title: Virgil van Dijk en Marseille-directeur reageren op transfer Timber
items.summary: Virgil van Dijk denkt dat Quinten Timber gaat slagen bij Olympique Marseille, dat de concurrentie te snel af wilde zijn door de 24-jarige middenvelder nu al over te nemen van Feyenoord.

items.title: Littler to face Van Veen in blockbuster Premier League Darts opener
items.summary: The Premier League Darts 2026 schedule has been confirmed, with Luke Littler meeting Gian van Veen on the opening night in a repeat of January's World Darts Championship final.

items.title: Women's League Cup to undergo name and format changes
items.summary: England's secondary cup competition will adopt a format like the Champions League from next season – and will "probably not" be called the League Cup.

items.title: Laura Holden: ‘I don’t want what happened to me to happen to anyone else’
items.summary: The former Aberdeen midfielder, now with Swindon, opens up about the debilitating effect of suffering an ACL injury during her time in Scotland “People need to know what happened,” Laura Holden says as she reflects on her difficult two years at Aberdeen when injury changed the course of her life. “It’s not all sunshine and roses. There are demons that just get brushed under the carpet without having the light shone on them.” It has taken the Swindon Town midfielder time and a change of club to process everything that happened in Scotland. Holden joined the Dons in August 2023, determined to establish herself as a key player at one of the biggest clubs in the Scottish Women’s Premier League. But just six matches and 31 minutes into the first season, she ruptured her anterior cruciate ligament away at Hibernian. This is an extract from our free email about women’s football, Moving the Goalposts. To get the full edition, visit this page and follow the instructions. Moving the Goalposts is delivered to your inboxes every Tuesday and Thursday. Continue reading...

items.title: John Herdman led Canada to a long-awaited World Cup. Can he do the same with Indonesia?
items.summary: The South Asian nation is among the world’s most populous and is mad for football, but qualifying for the World Cup is a big ask There’s a simple change of scenery, and then there’s John Herdman’s latest coaching move. The 50-year-old has taken quite a jump from Canada, a huge country where soccer is not the biggest sport, to Indonesia, a huge country where it definitely is. If he can repeat his 2022 heroics for 2030, he will be a hero to a nation of 280 million people who are just desperate to return to the global stage. Canada had a gap of 36 years between their first and second World Cup appearances. If the new coach in Jakarta is successful next time, then what will be a 92-year wait would come to an end. Continue reading...

items.title: 'Sorry she was mad' - Osaka claims heated win at Australian Open
items.summary: Naomi Osaka earned a heated Australian Open win over Sorana Cirstea as defending champion Madison Keys, Iga Swiatek and Amanda Anisimova all advanced.

items.title: Southampton’s Léo Scienza: ‘I am in the most difficult league in the world. It’s a bloodbath’
items.summary: Brazilian endured hard times in the Swedish fifth tier after his father’s death but has found a home in the Championship Seven years ago Léo Scienza’s life broke into a thousand small pieces. On his 20th birthday his father died and the young footballer locked himself in his room for two months, having lost the will to live. “You know when everything is bad and nothing makes sense any more?” the Southampton midfielder says. “My life had no meaning any more. “Look, everyone has a dark side and I’m not the best person to talk about depression or what depression is. In fact, I only understood it later. My father died on my birthday – that will always be marked in my life. After he died I just wanted to stay in my dark room doing nothing. I didn’t want to see anyone, I didn’t want to talk to anyone.” Continue reading...

items.title: Why are there so many goalless draws in the Premier League this season?
items.summary: Passes, shots and goals are all down on last season. It might keep tacticians happy but it’s not as much fun By Opta Analyst Gerard Piqué spoke to his former Spain teammate Iker Casillas on his podcast last February and the topic of goalless draws came up. You might expect a centre-back and goalkeeper to be excited about the art of defending but rather Piqué suggested that teams should be punished for participating in goalless draws. “It can’t be that you go to a football stadium, spend €100, €200 or €300, and the match ends 0-0,” said Piqué. “Something needs to change. One proposal to consider would be that if the match ends 0-0, the teams would score zero points. Then the match would open up in the 70th minute.” Continue reading...
